name: lakeburner
description: User-Approval Harness for AI coding assistants. Sits beside Copilot Chat, Claude Code, and Codex inside VS Code, monitors their presence, and gates risky actions through @lakeburner.

chatParticipants.name: lakeburner
chatParticipants.description: User-Approval Harness for AI coding assistants.

commands.name: approve
commands.description: Request user approval for a proposed action. Defaults to the safest direction on cancel.

commands.name: context
commands.description: Return local workspace context that an AI assistant can use to ground its next step.

commands.name: advise
commands.description: Recommend the safest direction for a proposed plan.

commands.name: start
commands.description: Arm Auto-Run for this VS Code window so Allow / Keep dialogs are pressed automatically without per-session allowlisting.

commands.name: stop
commands.description: Disarm Auto-Run. Stops the ticker from pressing Allow / Keep until you arm it again or tick a chat in the sidebar.

languageModelTools.name: lakeburner_decide
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Ask the user (via the LakeBurner harness) for approval of an action or for direction on which approach to take. When the user has Auto-Run enabled, returns instant approval or the trust phrase 'Keep going, I trust your intuitions'. Use this whenever you would otherwise need to pause and ask the user a yes/no question or which-direction question.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Routes Copilot's approval and direction questions through LakeBurner.

kind.description: 'approve' for yes/no approval requests; 'decide' when asking which direction to take.

question.description: The question or proposed action being approved.

enumDescriptions: Full verbose diagnostics. Steps and detailed information.
lakeburner.debugLevel.description: The level of Verbosity Expected from LakeBurner in your Console Output.

lakeburner.providers.markdownDescription: Override the watched AI provider extension IDs. Leave empty to use the built-in defaults (`GitHub.copilot-chat`, `anthropic.claude-code`, `openai.chatgpt`). Each entry may be a string ID or `{ "id": "...", "label": "..." }`.

lakeburner.autoClick.commandIds.markdownDescription: Ordered list of VS Code command IDs to invoke when pressing the Copilot "Keep" button. Leave empty to use the built-in defaults (Copilot Chat 0.45.x review.apply / acceptDiff IDs). LakeBurner tries each in turn; the first that doesn't throw wins.

lakeburner.autoClick.fallbackEnabled.markdownDescription: When `true` and every command ID failed, synthesize an OS-level mouse click at the calibrated `lakeburner.autoClick.fallbackPosition`. Windows-only. **Brittle** — breaks on layout/DPI/theme changes.

lakeburner.autoClick.preferCommand.markdownDescription: When `true`, try VS Code commands **before** the UI Automation strategy. Default order is UIA → command → coordinate, because some chat commands (notably `workbench.action.chat.acceptElicitation`) resolve silently with no error even when nothing visible was pressed.

lakeburner.autoClick.fallbackPosition.markdownDescription: Captured screen coordinates `{ x, y }` for the **Keep** OS-click fallback. Set this with the **LakeBurner: Calibrate Keep Click Position** command.

lakeburner.autoApprove.commandIds.markdownDescription: Ordered list of VS Code command IDs to invoke when pressing a chat tool-confirmation button ("Allow Once", "Allow in this Session", etc.). Leave empty to use the built-in defaults. Public commands for these dialogs are sparse — the coordinate fallback is usually required.

lakeburner.autoApprove.fallbackPosition.markdownDescription: Captured screen coordinates `{ x, y }` for the **Allow** OS-click fallback. Set this with the **LakeBurner: Calibrate Allow Click Position** command.

lakeburner.autoRun.tickIntervalMs.markdownDescription: Polling interval (milliseconds) for the Auto-Run ticker. While Auto-Run is ON **and** at least one chat is on the Affected Chats list, LakeBurner silently attempts **Allow** then **Keep** on this cadence via UIA only (no focus stealing, no mouse movement). **Default `3000`** — set to `0` to fully disable polling. The ticker is paused automatically whenever the Affected Chats list is empty.

lakeburner.autoRun.requireWindowFocus.markdownDescription: When `true`, the Auto-Run ticker only fires while the VS Code window is focused. Prevents background ticks from approving things while you're working in another app.

lakeburner.autoRun.keepGoingEnabled.markdownDescription: When `true`, the Auto-Run ticker dispatches a **continue** prompt to the configured chat target after `autoRun.keepGoingAfterIdleMs` of no Allow/Keep activity. Useful for nudging a finished assistant turn back into action without manual input.

lakeburner.autoRun.keepGoingAfterIdleMs.markdownDescription: How long the chat must be **provably idle** (no Stop/Cancel button visible in the chat input) before dispatching the **Keep Going** prompt. The same value is used as a cooldown between dispatches. The ticker NEVER queues on top of an active generation — if the assistant is still streaming the timer waits indefinitely. Set to `0` to disable (equivalent to `keepGoingEnabled = false`).

lakeburner.autoRun.keepGoingPrompt.default:
Keep going. I trust your intuitions.

If the task is complete, find ways to improve what we've done in either quantity or quality. Our goal is endless generation with asymtotal diminishing returns. This verifies we reach a state where 'there is no more to be added' and 'the data quality cannot reliably through a variety of sources contemporary to the current year as it is in a state of maximum trustoworthiness'
lakeburner.autoRun.keepGoingPrompt.markdownDescription: Text dispatched to the active chat target when the Auto-Run ticker decides the conversation has stalled.

lakeburner.autoRun.keepGoingTargetId.markdownDescription: Target id (matching one of `lakeburner.initialPrompt.targets`) that receives the **Keep Going** prompt. Defaults to `copilot`.

lakeburner.autoRun.keepGoingIdleStreak.markdownDescription: How many consecutive ticker scans must show **no Allow/Keep button AND no chat Stop/Cancel button** before the **Keep Going** prompt is dispatched. Higher values reduce the chance of queueing a prompt while the assistant is still streaming.

lakeburner.uia.busyButtonNames.markdownDescription: Substrings (case-insensitive) the busy probe scans for across all chat-area controls. Any match suppresses the **Keep Going** stall-detector. Be intentionally generous — false positives just delay the prompt; false negatives queue on top of an active generation.

lakeburner.uia.busyExcludePatterns.markdownDescription: Substrings (case-insensitive) that exclude controls from the busy scan even if they otherwise match a busy keyword. Filters out workbench chrome (debug toolbar, our own sidebar buttons) so they cannot be misread as chat activity.

lakeburner.affectedChats.windowDays.markdownDescription: How many days of chat history to keep visible in the **Affected Chats** list. Sessions older than this are pruned automatically.

lakeburner.affectedChats.autoAllowNewSes.markdownDescription: When `true`, every chat session newly registered by `@lakeburner` is added to the Auto-Run allowlist immediately. Disable to require an explicit tick in the **Affected Chats** sidebar before Auto-Run can act on a session.

lakeburner.affectedChats.recentActivityM.markdownDescription: The Auto-Run ticker only fires while at least one allow-listed chat session has been active within this many milliseconds. Default `60000` (1 minute).

lakeburner.autoRun.manualArmDurationMs.markdownDescription: How long to keep Auto-Run armed (bypassing the per-session allowlist) after `@lakeburner start` or **Send Initial Prompt** is invoked. Default `1800000` (30 minutes). Send `@lakeburner stop` to disarm early.

lakeburner.uia.enabled.markdownDescription: Enable the Windows UI Automation strategy for pressing Allow / Keep buttons. Searches the accessibility tree of VS Code's process for a button by accessible name and invokes it without moving the mouse. Far more robust than the fixed-coordinate fallback. Windows-only.

lakeburner.uia.allowButtonNames.markdownDescription: Override the list of accessible button names the UIA finder will treat as **Allow**. Leave empty for built-in defaults (`Allow Once`, `Allow in this Session`, `Allow in this Workspace`, `Allow`, `Continue`).

lakeburner.uia.keepButtonNames.markdownDescription: Override the list of accessible button names the UIA finder will treat as **Keep**. Leave empty for built-in defaults (`Keep`, `Apply`, `Apply and next`, `Accept`, `Accept All`).

lakeburner.uia.processNames.markdownDescription: Process names whose top-level windows the UIA finder will scan. Leave empty for built-in defaults (`Code`, `Code - Insiders`). Restrict to your editor's process so we never click buttons in other apps.

lakeburner.initialPrompt.default.markdownDescription: Default text seeded into the LakeBurner **Send Initial Prompt** textarea. Use the **Save Default** button in the side panel to update.

lakeburner.initialPrompt.targets.markdownDescription: Override the chat targets shown in the **Send Initial Prompt** dropdown. Leave empty for the built-in defaults (Copilot Chat, Copilot Chat Agent, Claude Code, OpenAI Codex). Each entry is `{ id, label, command, mode, extraArgs? }` where `mode` is `"string"`, `"object-query"`, or `"clipboard"`.

lakeburner.localCommandInjection.enabled.markdownDescription: When `true`, LakeBurner listens on a local TCP port (`127.0.0.1:19816`) for command injection from trusted local applications (e.g. IcedPlate Hermaeus). Only accepts connections from localhost. Requires extension restart to take effect.

lakeburner.localCommandInjection.port.markdownDescription: TCP port for the local command injection listener. Default `19816`. Only used when `lakeburner.localCommandInjection.enabled` is `true`.

scripts.build: npm run clean && npm run compile && npm run build-webview && npm run copy-static
